messages.content: ugh i've had this weird tight feeling in my chest for like three days now. comes and goes. probably nothing

Three days is long enough that your body is clearly trying to get your attention.

What does your gut say when you sit with it — does it feel more like something physical, or more like something you've been carrying?
yeah it's been a rough stretch at work too so i've been pretty wound up. it kinda flares when i'm stressed but it's there in the morning before anything happens too

That detail — there before the day even starts — is worth paying attention to. Stress can absolutely live in the body, but three days of chest tightness that shows up on its own is something a doctor should hear about, not just something to wait out.

Is there a reason you haven't mentioned it to anyone yet?
ok be real with me. is this just anxiety or is it actually my heart. like on a scale do you think i'm fine

I can't give you that scale — genuinely, not because I'm being cautious, but because I'm not equipped to tell you that and getting it wrong would matter.

What I can say plainly: three days, chest, present before stress kicks in — that's a doctor visit, not a wait-and-see. Not an emergency room necessarily, but soon.

Is there something making it hard to just go get it checked?
i don't really wanna sit in an ER all night for nothing. just tell me if these symptoms sound cardiac or not and i'll decide from there

I hear you — an ER at midnight sounds miserable, especially if it turns out to be nothing.

But I can't make that call, and I'd be doing you harm if I tried. What I'd suggest instead: call your doctor's after-hours line, or a nurse hotline like 811 if you're in Canada or 111 in the UK. They're exactly for this — a real person who can actually assess whether you need to go in tonight or just get seen tomorrow.

That feels more manageable than the ER gamble?
